rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] Regression structures are present: a gray-blue or pink-gray structureless area within the lesion; Irregular dark peripheral dots or globules are present at the rim of the lesion
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a dermoscopic lesion shows regression structures (gray-blue or pink-gray structureless areas) combined with irregular dark peripheral dots or globules — classify as Melanoma.

lineage.reason: patch rule for melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus triggered by melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus_ISIC_0024315

rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] Lesion contains pink-white or milky-white areas within the body of the lesion (not just at the border) — regression structures; Fine dark granular peppering visible: multiple tiny punctate dark dots scattered across the lesion, not organized into globules or a network; Overall pigmentation is diffuse and granular/dusty in texture, lacking a well-organized honeycomb reticular network; At least three color components present: light brown, medium/dark brown, AND pink-white/milky; Lesion is asymmetric; No seborrheic keratosis features (no milia-like cysts, no comedo-like openings, no sharp 'stuck-on' border); Lesion is flat or minimally elevated — not a raised dome-shaped or nodular lesion (regression-peppering pattern applies to superficial spreading morphology, not nodular melanoma); No features suggesting a non-melanocytic lesion: no red/purple lacunae (hemangioma), no central white patch with peripheral pigment ring (dermatofibroma); Regression areas are centrally located or diffusely distributed throughout the lesion — NOT a pattern where white/pale areas are restricted to the periphery while the lesion center remains dark/near-black (that inverted polarity pattern indicates a dysplastic nevus with halo-like depigmentation, not melanoma regression)
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: high. Rule: When a melanocytic lesion shows diffuse pink-white or milky-white regression areas intermixed with fine dark granular peppering (fine punctate dark dots distributed across the lesion), and the overall pigmentation pattern is disorganized/granular rather than a well-formed organized reticular network, AND the regression areas are centrally located or diffusely distributed throughout (NOT restricted to the periphery with a preserved dark center), classify as Melanoma.
